heaviest_rainfall.status: Ranked first for total rainfall, today's storm had precipitation totalling 46.39mm, falling at 2.2mm per hour at times during the 135.7 hour long storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 27.0mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: Today's rainstorm ranked highest on our record table for the length of the storm as it lasted 135.7 hours; it delivered 46.39mm of rain at a maximum rate of 2.2mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: A significant rainy period is on-going and is ranked 8th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 9 consecutive days beginning 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 6 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (178°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 51.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.2°C) and outside temperature (down 2.1°C) and more moisture-laden rainfall (up 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 343 m) and rainfall (down 0.2 mm) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 59.9m higher than yesterday, 92.8m lower than the past week, 342.7m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 561m minimum and 715m maximum, with an average of 671.5m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 764.3m and 1014.2m.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, humidity should not be a concern and little or no wind. Showers, fog or low cloud is expected, and weak sunlight because of clouds. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (96%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
